perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will retain Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement.', Recipient is not clear who is the 'Individual Consultant' referenced later on the contract, therefore creating uncertainty in the enforcement, creating an in-text contradiction. This makes the scope of permissible disclosure ambiguous, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees. Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows disclosure of confidential information to 'Individual Consultants' under specific conditions. The changed text omits this category, restricting disclosure only to 'other NatWest employees'. This omission creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which references 'Individual Consultants' without defining them within the changed context, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about who can access the confidential information. The changed text creates a contradiction, because the 'Individual Consultants' are not defined and creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer")', it removes a defined role that gives permission, therefore creating uncertainty in the enforcement, creating an in-text contradiction. This makes the scope of permissible disclosure ambiguous, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Recipient needs permission from InterTrust's Chairman or a Designated InterTrust Officer to disclose Confidential Information. The changed text removes this requirement, stating that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set forth immediately above, thus eliminating the need for permission and the role of the InterTrust officers. The contradicted text states, 'Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set forth immediately above,' which now lacks the crucial qualifier of needing permission, creating a contradiction with the original obligation and enforcement. By removing the permission requirement it creates ambiguity in the enforcement creating an in-text contradiction and this aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country.', the contract no longer specifies exceptions for copies done under explicit authorization. This creates contradiction within the section about confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. The Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for copies of Confidential Information with NatWest authorization. The changed text removes this exception, stating "The Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information." This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states "The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement." The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction by removing the possibility of authorized copies, which the contradicted text implies are allowed.

perturbation.original_text: Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.changed_text: Under no circumstances will any information be transferred pursuant to their agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.', this removes the clause regarding the possibility of exceptions for transfers of information. This contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. The Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information be transferred pursuant to their agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for information transfer under specific conditions (proper certification and notification). The changed text removes this allowance, stating 'Under no circumstances will any information be transferred pursuant to their agreement'. The contradicted text includes the same 'Under no circumstances will any information be transferred pursuant to their agreement' which now directly clashes with the implicit allowance that previously existed in the original text, thus establishing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage, for which damages may be impossible to measure or inadequate to compensate. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage, for which damages may be impossible to measure or inadequate to compensate. Accordingly,', it undermines the rationale of Intertrust obtaining an injunction. This creates an internal contradiction by weakening Intertrust's justification in the context of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever. Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened <PAGE> CONFIDENTIAL breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance under such circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that InterTrust may suffer substantial damage from unauthorized use of Confidential Information, providing a rationale for seeking an injunction. The changed text removes this rationale. The contradicted text allows InterTrust to obtain an injunction if a breach occurs. By removing the justification for the injunction, the modified text creates a contradiction because it weakens InterTrust's position to obtain an injunction. While the right to obtain an injunction remains, the removal of the 'substantial damage' acknowledgment undermines the enforceability and justification in the context of the agreement, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, the Recipient shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a court order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law; provided that the undersigned -------- ---- shall provide prompt advance notice thereof to InterTrust to enable InterTrust to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent such disclosure and shall disclose no more than the minimum information required by such court order or by law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a court order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law; provided that the undersigned -------- ---- shall disclose no more than the minimum information required by such court order or by law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'shall provide prompt advance notice thereof to InterTrust to enable InterTrust to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent such disclosure and', it contradicts the spirit of the confidentiality agreement, creating uncertainty in its enforcement and creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement, or NatWest's or any other NatWest employee's, obligations of confidentiality to NatWest and/or to InterTrust; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, as evidenced through written documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, as demonstrated through written documentation; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality to InterTrust or NatWest (as applicable) as of the date such information is so supplied. In addition, the Recipient shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a court order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law; provided that the undersigned -------- ---- shall disclose no more than the minimum information required by such court order or by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the Recipient 'shall provide prompt advance notice' to InterTrust, allowing them to seek protective measures before disclosure under a court order. The changed text removes this obligation. The contradicted text allows disclosure pursuant to a court order, but the original provision (now removed) ensured InterTrust had an opportunity to protect their confidential information. By removing the notice provision, the changed text creates a direct contradiction because InterTrust no longer has a mechanism to prevent or limit disclosure, contradicting the implied intent of maintaining confidentiality as much as possible, even under court order. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction as defined.
